title: NLAH Execution Contract
description: Natural-Language Agent Harness contract schema for ACOS skills. Defines required outputs, budgets, completion conditions, permissions, and artifact configuration. Based on arXiv:2603.25723.

required-outputs.description: Artifacts that must be produced for the harness run to be considered complete.

type.description: Output type. 'file' = written to disk, 'artifact' = placed in artifact dir, 'stdout' = printed to user, 'state' = persisted in manifest.

description.description: What this output contains and why it matters.

optional.description: If true, missing output triggers a warning instead of a failure.

completion-conditions.description: Natural-language conditions that must all be true for the harness to declare completion. Evaluated by the in-loop LLM against current state.

budget.description: Resource constraints for this harness run.

max-tool-calls.description: Maximum number of tool invocations before budget warning.

context-budget.description: Fraction of context window this skill should consume (0.0-1.0).

timeout-minutes.description: Wall-clock timeout for the entire harness run.

permissions.description: Permission scope for this harness. Composes with agent-iam.json via intersection semantics.

additional-paths.description: Additional glob patterns for allowed file paths.

denied-paths.description: Glob patterns for explicitly denied file paths.

can-spawn-agents.description: Whether this harness may spawn child agents.

can-modify-harness.description: Whether this harness may modify its own SKILL.md.

artifacts.description: Durable artifact configuration for execution state persistence.

state-dir.description: Template path for artifact storage. Supports ${skill-name} and ${session-id} variables.

manifest.description: Filename for the artifact manifest within state-dir.

execution-log.description: Filename for the append-only execution log.

verification.description: How outputs are verified before the harness declares success.

method.description: Verification method. 'existence' = files exist and non-empty, 'content-check' = LLM validates content against description, 'script' = run a verification script, 'llm-judge' = LLM scores output quality.
